items.text: crates/liminal/Cargo.toml exists with beamr as a dependency.
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs re-exports channel, conversation, envelope, and error modules.
ChannelMode enum has Ephemeral and Durable variants.
ChannelConfig requires name, schema, and mode — no silent defaults.
ChannelHandle provides publish, subscribe, and close operations.
Channel actor is a beamr process owning its subscriber list, schema, and lifecycle state.
Channel actor delivers published messages to all matching subscribers.
Channel actor is supervised — crash restarts the individual channel without affecting others.
Ephemeral channel has zero persistence overhead — no haematite calls.
Subscription handle allows unsubscribe.
Subscriber removal on process death is automatic via beamr linking.
Subscription supports optional predicate filter on message content.
Schema type defines the expected message structure for a channel.
Publish rejects messages that do not match the channel's declared schema.
Schema validation happens at publish time, before any subscriber sees the message.
Schema evolution (add field with default) is supported without subscriber redeployment.
Envelope carries payload, causal context, schema identifier, publisher identity, and timestamp.
Causal context contains optional parent message reference for ordering.
Envelope is the unit of routing, persistence, and delivery throughout the system.
Conversation actor is a supervised beamr process.
Conversation actor is linked to its participants via beamr process links.
Participant crash is detected immediately via process linking — no heartbeat.
ConversationConfig specifies participants, timeout, and durability mode.
ConversationHandle exposes send, receive, and close operations.
Conversation state machine tracks current phase and accumulated context.
Request-reply pattern: send request, receive response, conversation closes.
Streaming pattern: one party sends a sequence, backpressure mediated by conversation.
Dispatch pattern: select a consumer, link, observe completion, route to another on crash.
LiminalError enum covers channel, conversation, schema, and delivery failure cases.
LiminalError implements std::error::Error and Debug.
